fundamentals.name: Netflix, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Netflix, Inc. provides entertainment services worldwide. The company offers television (TV) series, documentaries, feature films, games, and live programming across various genres and languages. It also provides members the ability to receive streaming content through a host of internet-connected devices, including TVs, digital video players, TV set-top boxes, and mobile devices. Netflix, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Los Gatos, California.

judgment.summary: Strongly growing company with high margins and robust free cash flow, trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 1.4%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 16.2% YoY and earnings growth 86.4% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin of 28.5% and operating margin of 32.3% demonstrate excellent profitability.
Free cash flow of $25.99B TTM is substantial and supports business operations.
Forward P/E of 22.39x is attractive given the growth and profitability metrics.
High return on equity of 48.5% shows efficient use of shareholder capital.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 53.79% indicates moderate leverage.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 1.4%.
PEG ratio of 1.70 suggests growth may be partially priced in.

news.paragraph: Netflix is seeing mixed analyst sentiment, with some upgrading the stock and predicting a significant comeback, while others maintain a bearish stance, believing it's still in a bear market. This divergence follows a recent quarter where earnings slightly missed estimates, though revenue and earnings growth remain strong year-over-year. The stock's performance relative to other entertainment companies is also under scrutiny.
